text: Auth migration: replace legacy Express/Redis session middleware with Auth0-issued OAuth 2.0 access and refresh tokens (Authorization Code flow with PKCE). Owner: Nuha. Status: In Progress. Currently blocked on refresh-token rotation work tracked in ENG-419 before prod rollout.

Status as of Fri Jun 5: OAuth callback and token exchange shipped Tuesday (PR #847), deployed to staging Wednesday — SSO sign-in works there. Ivan reviewed the spec Wednesday and flagged we need sliding-window refresh-token rotation with reuse detection before prod. Tracking that as ENG-419, mid-implementation now. Frontend PKCE flow (ENG-418, Jamie) progressing in parallel. Target prod rollout: Tue Jun 9.

Security review note: the current implementation issues long-lived refresh tokens with no rotation. Before prod, we need sliding-window refresh-token rotation with reuse detection — each /refresh call must issue a new token and revoke the old one; if a stale refresh is ever replayed, invalidate the whole token family. Happy to pair on the design.

Implement /auth/callback endpoint: exchange the Auth0 authorization code plus PKCE verifier for access and refresh tokens, then set an httpOnly session cookie. Status: Done. Merged in PR #847 on Tue Jun 2; staging deploy Wed Jun 3, smoke tests passed.

React frontend: generate the PKCE code_verifier and challenge, redirect to Auth0 /authorize, handle the callback redirect back to /auth/callback. Owner: Jamie. Status: In Progress. Runs in parallel with ENG-419 — does not block prod rollout on its own.

ref: ENG-419 Refresh-token rotation w/ reuse detection
text: Implement sliding-window refresh-token rotation per Ivan's Jun 3 security review. Each /refresh issues a new refresh token and revokes the old one. Track token family lineage in the DB; if a revoked refresh is ever replayed, invalidate the whole family. Owner: Nuha. Status: In Progress, started Thu Jun 4. Target: ready for Ivan's review by Fri Jun 5 EOD. Blocks ENG-412 prod rollout.
Map existing session-based users to Auth0 user IDs during the migration window so logged-in users don't lose their sessions when we cut over. Status: Todo. Blocked on ENG-412 prod rollout.
Automate Auth0 token-signing key rotation (currently a manual annual process). Status: Backlog / stretch. Not blocking the migration; can ship next quarter.

Reviewed the OAuth migration spec — looks solid overall. One required change before prod: we need sliding-window refresh-token rotation with reuse detection. Long-lived refresh tokens are a real risk if one leaks. Left a detailed comment on ENG-412.

OAuth migration status — callback handler shipped Tuesday and is healthy in staging. Ivan flagged that refresh-token rotation needs sliding-window plus reuse detection; I'm building that under ENG-419 today, should be ready for review by EOD. Prod rollout target slipped to Tue Jun 9.

ref: Cal 2026-06-03 14:00 — OAuth Security Review w/ Ivan
text: Held: OAuth Security Review with Ivan Chubs (Security) and Nuha. Outcome: Ivan approved overall approach but required refresh-token rotation with reuse detection before prod. Action: Nuha to scope as separate ticket — became ENG-419.
